source_jobs.title: Social Content Manager
source_jobs.description:
This role is on-site in San Francisco in the Dogpatch neighborhood.




ABOUT GIGA

Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and is working with Fortune 500 customers to deploy the next generation of customer experience - real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world's largest enterprises.

Industry leaders like DoorDash trust Giga with their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email, in high-stakes regulated environments where accuracy and compliance matter. We're at an exciting inflection point.

While we've found real commercial success, our ambitions are larger: to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. The work affects millions of people every day, and our team has the autonomy to make true impact - with brilliant founders, a clear path forward, and the kind of momentum that defines generational companies.

If being part of that resonates with you, we'd love to hear from you!

 - Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A https://fortune.com/2025/11/05/voice-ai-giga-raise-61-million-customer-service-series-a/

 - DoorDash and Giga Partnership https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7391576181009018883/




ABOUT THE ROLE

Engineering-first AI companies don't get attention by accident. They get it because someone is highly online, posting deliberately, day after day. We're hiring a Social Content Manager to own X and LinkedIn end-to-end: the posts, the POV, and the measurement of what resonates. The judgment lives with you — this is not a brand-voice-by-committee role.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Daily content output across X and LinkedIn (50%): Original posts, threads, replies, comment-baiting on adjacent accounts, and derivative drops from flagship engineering posts on a weekly cadence. Stack: native tooling + AI-assisted drafting. Quality control: every post tied to a hypothesis (why this hook, this audience, this hour) and tracked against it.

 - Editorial POV & voice ownership (30%): What Giga does post, what we explicitly don't, how we sound. A short list of content we don't ship and the specific, technical, slightly contrarian stance that separates us from generic AI thought leadership.

 - Performance learning & iteration (20%): Weekly readout on what worked and what didn't, fed back into the editorial calendar. Measured on engagement quality (saves, replies from target accounts, profile visits from named companies) over vanity volume.




WHO YOU ARE

You are highly online — you can name three accounts that posted something interesting this week and explain why. You've built a social presence (your own or a brand's) that earned attention without buying it. You ship at high cadence and have opinions on what makes B2B SaaS social bad. You can find the post hidden inside a customer email, a sales call transcript, or a Slack thread. You move fast with AI tools but the judgment is yours.




WHERE YOU MIGHT COME FROM

In-house social leads at engineering-first companies (Vercel, Linear, Anthropic, Notion). Indie creators or writers with a tech audience. Founders or early operators who built a personal brand worth following. Earlier-career candidates who are exceptionally sharp, fast, and digitally fluent.




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Catered lunch daily + dinner stipend

 - $150/month wellness benefit (gym, fitness classes, mental health)

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k) plan

 - Paid parental leave (12 weeks maternal, 6 weeks paternal)

 - Commuter benefits



Giga is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, or veteran status.




HOW TO APPLY

Send links to three things you've published online that you're proud of. One sentence on why each.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer I / II
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GIGA

Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and has several paying customers, including DoorDash. We’re building the next generation of customer experience — real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world’s largest enterprises.

It’s an exciting inflection point for the company. While we have been successful, we have larger ambitions. Our goal is to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. To achieve this, we need more great engineers.

The work affects millions of people every day and our engineers have autonomy and make true impact. This opportunity is unique because we have brilliant founders, have found commercial success, and see a clear path to becoming a generational company. Some further info about us:

 - Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A https://fortune.com/2025/11/05/voice-ai-giga-raise-61-million-customer-service-series-a/

 - DoorDash and Giga Partnership https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7391576181009018883/

Giga builds AI agents trusted by the largest B2C companies in the world. Industry leaders like DoorDash trust Giga with their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email. If being a part of this resonates with you, please apply!




THE ROLE

We're looking for software engineers to help build the systems that power our AI agents. You'll work across the backend, from data pipelines and integrations to agent infrastructure, shipping features alongside experienced engineers who will help you grow.

This is a role where you'll contribute to real problems from day one. We expect you to ramp up quickly, take ownership of your work, and operate with increasing independence as you learn the codebase.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

You'll contribute to projects across our stack. Some examples:

 - Atlas: Building features for our AI assistant: charts and alerts in Slack, natural language queries, and expanding Atlas to manage platform resources

 - Activity Stream: Log visualization with filters, timestamps, and frequency charts to give visibility into agent behavior

 - Dynamic knowledge: Adding time-based knowledge (like ongoing incidents) that auto-updates from sources like status pages

 - Agent memory: Conversation awareness and recent interaction lookups so agents remember context across sessions

As an early-career engineer, you'll be paired with senior engineers on larger initiatives while also owning smaller projects end-to-end as you ramp up.




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU

 - Have 1-3 years of professional software engineering experience

 - Are curious and ask good questions—you want to understand why things work, not just how

 - Can take feedback well and iterate quickly

 - Prefer shipping over perfection but still care about quality

 - Want to grow at a startup where you'll have real responsibility early




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Competitive base + bonus + equity

 - Catered lunch daily

 - Dinner stipend

 - $500/month wellness & commuter benefit (gym, fitness classes, mental health)

 - 401(k) plan

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage

Giga is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

We're seeking a Forward Deployed Engineer to join our San Francisco team. You'll serve as the technical interface and project leader between Giga and our customers, building and deploying sophisticated AI-driven solutions at scale.

 - Strong Background: Computer science degree or industry-equivalent experience and customer facing experience (e.g. consulting); 4+ years of total managerial experience.

 - Entrepreneurial: Preference for candidates with experience at successful startups - former founders are especially valued; ability to context switch constantly and rapidly; high bar for quality work.

 - Gritty: Capable of working long productive hours; very high attention to detail; can own a project end-to-end with limited or no guidance.

 - Experienced: 4+ years customer facing experience; 4+ years of hands-on engineering experience.

Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and is working with Fortune 500 customers to deploy the next generation of customer experience - real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world's largest enterprises.

Industry leaders like DoorDash trust Giga with their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email, in high-stakes regulated environments where accuracy and compliance matter. We're at an exciting inflection point.

While we've found real commercial success, our ambitions are larger: to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. The work affects millions of people every day, and our team has the autonomy to make true impact - with brilliant founders, a clear path forward, and the kind of momentum that defines generational companies.

If being part of that resonates with you, we'd love to hear from you!

 - Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A https://fortune.com/2025/11/05/voice-ai-giga-raise-61-million-customer-service-series-a/

 - DoorDash and Giga Partnership https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7391576181009018883/




THE ROLE

We're looking for backend engineers to build the infrastructure and systems that power our AI agents. You'll work across the stack, from data pipelines and analytics to real-time agent systems, shipping features that directly impact how our customers operate.

This isn't a role where you'll be handed a spec and asked to execute. You'll own problems end-to-end: figuring out the right approach, building it, and iterating based on what you learn.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

A few examples from our current roadmap:

 - Analytics rewrite: Migrating our analytics service to ClickHouse and rebuilding it to scale through our next 10x of growth

 - Hallucination detection: Improving our detection systems with the goal of driving hallucination rates toward zero

 - Browser use agent: Scaling, stabilizing, and adding features and accuracy to our browser use agent in lieu of formal customer APIs

As a senior engineer, you'll be expected to make sound technical decisions, ship reliably, and help set the bar for code quality and system design. You'll have significant autonomy and influence over what gets built and how.




TECH STACK

 - Backend: Python (Django/FastAPI), TypeScript (Node.js)

 - Infrastructure: AWS, Modal, Kubernetes, Postgres

 - Frontend: TypeScript, Next.js (you'll interact with this, but it's not the focus)




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU

 - Have 4+ years building backend systems, ideally at companies where reliability and scale mattered

 - Are comfortable with ambiguity: you can take a loosely-defined problem and figure out what to build

 - You'll jump into unfamiliar parts of the codebase when that's what the problem requires

 - Write clean code and care about getting the details right, but also know when to ship

 - Want to work on hard problems with a small team where your work has obvious impact

 - Are excited about AI agents and the infrastructure challenges that come with running them in production




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Competitive base + bonus + equity

 - Catered lunch daily

 - Dinner stipend

 - $500/month wellness & commuter benefit (gym, fitness classes, mental health)

 - 401(k) plan

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage

We're looking for a staff engineer to help shape the technical direction of our backend systems. You'll lead complex projects, make architectural decisions that affect the whole platform, and raise the bar for engineering quality across the team.

WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

You'll drive technical initiatives across the company. Some examples:

 - System architecture: Designing how our agent infrastructure should evolve as we scale, making tradeoffs between speed, reliability, and complexity

 - Technical strategy: Identifying gaps in our systems before they become problems and building the case for addressing them

 - Engineering quality: Setting standards for code review, system design, and operational excellence through your own work

At the staff level, you'll work across team boundaries and be responsible for outcomes beyond your individual output. You'll mentor other engineers and help them grow, but through collaboration on real work rather than formal management.

 - Infrastructure: AWS, Modal, Kubernetes, Postgres

 - Frontend: TypeScript, Next.js (you'll interact with this when needed)

 - Have 7+ years of backend engineering experience, with a track record of leading complex projects

 - Have opinions about how systems should be built, and can articulate the tradeoffs behind those opinions

 - Can take a vague problem and turn it into a clear technical approach without much guidance

 - Write excellent code and hold others to a high standard without being a bottleneck

 - Are energized by hard problems and comfortable with uncertainty

 - Want to be hands-on at a company where staff engineers ship code, not just review it

 - Have built systems that scaled and understand what breaks at each order of magnitude

ABOUT GIGA

Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and is working with Fortune 500 customers to deploy the next generation of customer experience - real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world's largest enterprises.

Industry leaders like DoorDash trust Giga with their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email, in high-stakes regulated environments where accuracy and compliance matter. We're at an exciting inflection point.

While we've found real commercial success, our ambitions are larger: to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. The work affects millions of people every day, and our team has the autonomy to make true impact - with brilliant founders, a clear path forward, and the kind of momentum that defines generational companies.

If being part of that resonates with you, we'd love to hear from you!

 - Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A https://fortune.com/2025/11/05/voice-ai-giga-raise-61-million-customer-service-series-a/

 - DoorDash and Giga Partnership https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7391576181009018883/


MORE ABOUT THE ROLE

We're seeking an Agent Engineer to join our San Francisco team. You'll serve as a technical interface between Giga and our customers, building and deploying sophisticated solutions.



WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Assisting customer engagements from start to end: run discovery calls & demos, build/maintain world class agents, participate in customer calls, and serve as the primary PoC in a fast-paced environment.

 - Own the full agent development life cycle: build and prototype quickly, set up CI/CD, monitor live usage, iterate to targets, debug live issues, communicate with customers, and document best practices to speed up future projects.

 - Close the feedback loop with product & platform: capture unmet needs, prototype new features, contribute directly to the codebase, and collaborate with core teams to strengthen the platform for all customers.



WHO YOU ARE / MUST-HAVES:

 - Know the basics: Understanding of React, Python, tool calls, APIs, etc.

 - Interpersonal Acumen: Able to solve problems in a people-centric manner and communicate effectively, with experience/interest in customer-facing roles.

 - Entrepreneurial: Preference for candidates with aspirations who want to be founders or leaders; ability to context switch constantly and rapidly; high bar for quality work.

 - Gritty: Capable of working long productive hours, customer focused, very high attention to detail, and obsession to deliver high-quality work.

 - Coachable: Be able to take in feedback and adapt at a quick pace.

 - Experience: 1+ years of experience; computer science, analytics, or consultant background strongly preferred.

 


PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Competitive base + bonus + equity

 - Catered lunch daily

 - Dinner stipend

 - $500/month wellness & commuter benefit (gym, fitness classes, mental health)

 - 401(k) plan

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage

---

Giga is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GIGA

Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and is working with Fortune 500 customers to deploy the next generation of customer experience - real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world's largest enterprises.

Industry leaders like DoorDash trust Giga with their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email, in high-stakes regulated environments where accuracy and compliance matter. We're at an exciting inflection point.

While we've found real commercial success, our ambitions are larger: to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. The work affects millions of people every day, and our team has the autonomy to make true impact - with brilliant founders, a clear path forward, and the kind of momentum that defines generational companies.

If being part of that resonates with you, we'd love to hear from you!

 - Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A https://fortune.com/2025/11/05/voice-ai-giga-raise-61-million-customer-service-series-a/

 - DoorDash and Giga Partnership https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7391576181009018883/




ROLE OVERVIEW

We're looking for a Product Manager to help us achieve our goal of automating 98% of enterprise support and operations workflows. You'll own critical parts of our AI agent platform—building the products that enable self-improving agents to handle increasingly complex scenarios without human intervention.

You'll work at the intersection of cutting-edge AI capabilities and enterprise-grade product development: defining how agents learn from every interaction, orchestrating multi-party workflows across voice and chat, and expanding our platform into regulated industries like healthcare and financial services.

This role requires someone who can think deeply about AI product strategy while also diving into the technical details of real-time orchestration, emotional intelligence, and enterprise compliance.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN


SELF-IMPROVING AGENT PLATFORM

 - Drive the product strategy for agents that automatically improve from 60% to 98% resolution

 - Define how agents learn from every interaction—pattern recognition, policy refinement, edge case handling

 - Build feedback loops that identify gaps and automatically expand agent capabilities

 - Own the path from "agent handles simple cases" to "agent handles 98% of all cases"


MULTI-PARTY ORCHESTRATION

 - Lead product strategy for complex real-time workflows (e.g., coordinating customer, driver, and merchant simultaneously)

 - Define how agents make decisions by cross-referencing multiple data sources, policies, and external systems in real-time

 - Own the sub-500ms latency experience across voice, chat, and email channels

 - Drive emotional intelligence features—sentiment detection, tone matching, natural conversation flow


AGENT BUILDER & CONSOLE

 - Define the product vision for our no-code/low-code agent configuration platform

 - Own the policy engine, scenario builder, and custom code execution capabilities

 - Drive improvements to knowledge base management and RAG integrations

 - Shape the developer experience for teams building and customizing agents


EXPANSION INTO REGULATED INDUSTRIES

 - Partner with healthcare, financial services, and compliance-heavy customers to understand their unique requirements

 - Define product capabilities for high-compliance environments (audit trails, data privacy, regulatory adherence)

 - Own integrations strategy (Zendesk, Salesforce, industry-specific systems)

 - Drive the 2-week enterprise deployment goal that differentiates us from competitors




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


PRODUCT STRATEGY & EXECUTION

 - Define and execute the roadmap toward 98% automation of support tickets and operations workflows

 - Make hard prioritization decisions: which agent capabilities unlock the next 10% of automated resolution?

ABOUT GIGA

Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and has several paying customers, including DoorDash. We’re building the next generation of customer experience — real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world’s largest enterprises.

It’s an exciting inflection point for the company. While we have been successful, we have larger ambitions. Our goal is to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. To achieve this, we need more great engineers.

The work affects millions of people every day and our engineers have autonomy and make true impact. This opportunity is unique because we have brilliant founders, have found commercial success, and see a clear path to becoming a generational company. Some further info about us:

 - Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A https://fortune.com/2025/11/05/voice-ai-giga-raise-61-million-customer-service-series-a/

 - DoorDash and Giga Partnership https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7391576181009018883/

Giga builds AI agents trusted by the largest B2C companies in the world. Industry leaders like DoorDash trust Giga with their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email. If being a part of this resonates with you, please apply!




THE ROLE

We're looking for a staff engineer to help shape the technical direction of our backend systems. You'll lead complex projects, make architectural decisions that affect the whole platform, and raise the bar for engineering quality across the team.

This is a role for someone who wants to build, not just advise. You'll write code, own systems, and ship, but you'll also be expected to think beyond your immediate work to identify what the team should be building next and how we should build it.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

You'll drive technical initiatives across the company. Some examples:

 - System architecture: Designing how our agent infrastructure should evolve as we scale, making tradeoffs between speed, reliability, and complexity

 - Hard technical problems: Owning the most ambiguous, highest-stakes projects where getting it wrong would hurt the business

 - Technical strategy: Identifying gaps in our systems before they become problems and building the case for addressing them

 - Engineering quality: Setting standards for code review, system design, and operational excellence through your own work

At the staff level, you'll work across team boundaries and be responsible for outcomes beyond your individual output. You'll mentor other engineers and help them grow, but through collaboration on real work rather than formal management.




TECH STACK

 - Backend: Python (Django/FastAPI), TypeScript (Node.js)

 - Infrastructure: AWS, Modal, Kubernetes, Postgres

 - Frontend: TypeScript, Next.js (you'll interact with this when needed)




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU

 - Have 7+ years of backend engineering experience, with a track record of leading complex projects

 - Have opinions about how systems should be built, and can articulate the tradeoffs behind those opinions

 - Can take a vague problem and turn it into a clear technical approach without much guidance

 - Write excellent code and hold others to a high standard without being a bottleneck

 - Are energized by hard problems and comfortable with uncertainty

 - Want to be hands-on at a company where staff engineers ship code, not just review it

 - Have built systems that scaled and understand what breaks at each order of magnitude
   
   


PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Competitive base + bonus + equity

 - Catered lunch daily

 - Dinner stipend

 - $500/month wellness & commuter benefit (gym, fitness classes, mental health)

 - 401(k) plan

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GIGA

Giga has recently raised a $61M Series A and is working with Fortune 500 customers to deploy the next generation of customer experience - real-time AI agents that can understand emotion, resolve issues instantly, and scale across the world's largest enterprises.

Industry leaders like DoorDash trust Giga with their most complex support and operations workflows across voice, chat, and email, in high-stakes regulated environments where accuracy and compliance matter. We're at an exciting inflection point.

While we've found real commercial success, our ambitions are larger: to become the go-to AI platform for all enterprise automation, powered by our voice superintelligence. The work affects millions of people every day, and our team has the autonomy to make true impact - with brilliant founders, a clear path forward, and the kind of momentum that defines generational companies.

If being part of that resonates with you, we'd love to hear from you!

 - Voice AI startup Giga raises $61M Series A https://fortune.com/2025/11/05/voice-ai-giga-raise-61-million-customer-service-series-a/

 - DoorDash and Giga Partnership https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7391576181009018883/




THE ROLE

We're looking for backend engineers to build the infrastructure and systems that power our AI agents. You'll work across the stack, from data pipelines and analytics to real-time agent systems, shipping features that directly impact how our customers operate.

This isn't a role where you'll be handed a spec and asked to execute. You'll own problems end-to-end: figuring out the right approach, building it, and iterating based on what you learn.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

A few examples from our current roadmap:

 - Analytics rewrite: Migrating our analytics service to ClickHouse and rebuilding it to scale through our next 10x of growth

 - Hallucination detection: Improving our detection systems with the goal of driving hallucination rates toward zero

 - Browser use agent: Scaling, stabilizing, and adding features and accuracy to our browser use agent in lieu of formal customer APIs

As a senior engineer, you'll be expected to make sound technical decisions, ship reliably, and help set the bar for code quality and system design. You'll have significant autonomy and influence over what gets built and how.




TECH STACK

 - Backend: Python (Django/FastAPI), TypeScript (Node.js)

 - Infrastructure: AWS, Modal, Kubernetes, Postgres

 - Frontend: TypeScript, Next.js (you'll interact with this, but it's not the focus)




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU

 - Have 4+ years building backend systems, ideally at companies where reliability and scale mattered

 - Are comfortable with ambiguity: you can take a loosely-defined problem and figure out what to build

 - You'll jump into unfamiliar parts of the codebase when that's what the problem requires

 - Write clean code and care about getting the details right, but also know when to ship

 - Want to work on hard problems with a small team where your work has obvious impact

 - Are excited about AI agents and the infrastructure challenges that come with running them in production




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Competitive base + bonus + equity

 - Catered lunch daily

 - Dinner stipend

 - $500/month wellness & commuter benefit (gym, fitness classes, mental health)

 - 401(k) plan

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage



Giga is an equal opportunity employer. We're committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

THE ROLE

We're looking for a security engineer to build the systems and practices that keep our platform and our customers' data safe. Our AI agents handle sensitive customer interactions at scale, which means security isn't a side concern here. It's core to the product.

This is a hands-on engineering role, not a compliance-checklist role. You'll write code, build tooling, and design systems while also shaping how the rest of the team thinks about security. You'll be the person who makes it easy to build things securely, not the person who slows everyone down.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

A few examples from our current priorities:

 - Application security: Securing the platform end-to-end, from the APIs our customers use to configure agents to the systems that handle real-time conversations and customer data

 - Access controls and authorization: Building role-based access controls, permissions models, and audit logging so our enterprise customers can manage their teams with confidence

 - Security tooling and automation: Building the infrastructure for vulnerability scanning, dependency auditing, secrets management, and automated detection so security scales with the team

As a senior security engineer, you'll own security across the stack and have significant influence over how we architect systems. You'll work closely with backend and infrastructure engineers to make sure security is baked into our platform from the start.




TECH STACK

 - Backend: Python (Django/FastAPI), TypeScript (Node.js)

 - Infrastructure: AWS, Modal, Kubernetes, Postgres

 - Tooling: Terraform, Docker, CI/CD pipelines




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU

 - Have 4+ years of experience in security engineering, with a strong software engineering foundation

 - Have secured production systems handling sensitive data and understand the difference between theoretical risks and real ones

 - Can write production-quality code, not just review it: you'll build security tooling and features, not just file tickets

 - Think about security as an enabler, not a blocker: you make it easy for other engineers to do the right thing

 - Are comfortable across application security, infrastructure security, and cloud security

 - Are excited about the unique security challenges of AI agents: prompt injection, data leakage, and problems that don't have established playbooks yet

THE ROLE

We're looking for a QA lead to own quality across our AI agent platform and run a distributed testing team. You'll define what "ready to ship" looks like, build the test strategies and processes from the ground up, and be the person in SF who keeps quality on track as the team scales.

This is a hands-on QA role. You'll write test plans, execute testing, and manage a team of offshore QA testers. You'll work closely with engineering and product to understand what's being built, identify where things can break, and make sure nothing ships until it's been thoroughly validated. If you've run QA for a fast-moving product team and know how to keep quality high without slowing everything down, this is your role.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

A few examples of what this role looks like day-to-day:

 - Test strategy and planning: Defining test plans, acceptance criteria, and coverage matrices for new features across our voice, chat, and email AI agents

 - AI agent validation: Testing agent conversations for accuracy, tone, hallucinations, and edge cases — figuring out how to systematically QA outputs that are different every time

 - Release management: Owning the QA gate for releases, coordinating test cycles, managing regression suites, and signing off on production readiness

 - Team management: Running a distributed QA team across SF and offshore locations — assigning work, reviewing results, setting standards, and keeping the team aligned across time zones

 - Bug triage and reporting: Classifying, prioritizing, and clearly documenting defects so engineering can reproduce and fix issues efficiently

 - Process design: Building the QA processes, workflows, and documentation that let us scale testing as the product and team grow

As a senior QA lead, you'll be the core owner of ensuring quality releases in the company. You'll have real influence over release cadence, quality standards, and how QA fits into the development lifecycle.
